beats.narration_text: Talofa — Liam, in for Bear. dance-video: you give Claude a danceable song and a character, and it builds a beat-synced reel where that character dances on the beat. Long downbeat-aligned beats — ten to fifteen seconds — driven by Seedance 2.0 from an audio slice. Today: anatomy, pipeline, how it works.

props.runningText: analyze beats → segment → Seedance audio-locked → assemble...
output: Seedance 2.0: still + audio slice → on-beat motion
Gate 1: verify one beat before the full run

beats.narration_text: Anatomy. One character. One song. Beats of ten to fifteen seconds, each ending on a musical downbeat — never longer, because Seedance's audio clip cap is fifteen seconds. The prompt is mostly choreography: character look, the dance move, how it hits the beat, what the camera does, and what the background space actually is. Style is a short tag. That is the whole anatomy.

props.title: Character + song → beat-synced dance reel.
props.calloutSub: downbeat grid is the master clock

beats.narration_text: Pipeline. Phase one: librosa finds the downbeat grid. Phase two: segment the song into beats of up to fifteen seconds, each ending on a downbeat; assign a move and a distinct camera to each; write the four-part prompt. Phase three: slice the master audio per beat. Phase four: Seedance — still plus audio slice, audio-locked motion. Phase five: concatenate clips, mux the master track.

props.title: Analyze → segment → slice → Seedance → assemble.

phases.label: ANALYZE
phases.note: analyze_audio.py → beat_data.json (real downbeat grid via librosa)

phases.label: SEGMENT
phases.note: build_dance_beatsheet.py → 10–15s beats, move + camera + 4-part prompt per beat

phases.label: SEEDANCE
phases.note: generate_video_seedance.sh — still + audio slice → on-beat clip per beat

phases.label: ASSEMBLE
phases.note: concat silent clips + mux master WAV (clips are generate_audio false by design)

props.footerNote: Gate 1: test one beat and verify the dance lands on the beat before the full run.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo. Phase two is the free phase — no audio, no video, no Higgsfield. Watch Claude write three dance beats for a rubber-hose Calloway character to a one-twenty B-P-M swing track. Character stated once. Three moves. Three distinct cameras. Background explicit.

output: Character: Cab Calloway rubber-hose puppet, white zoot suit
3 beats × CHARACTER + DANCE + CAMERA + BACKGROUND + STYLE
Phase 2 only — no Higgsfield, no audio

beats.narration_text: Beat one — fifteen seconds. A tall lanky grinning rubber-hose puppet in a white zoot suit. Dance: Calloway strut — glides side to side, head bobbing, snaps a finger on every downbeat. Camera: locked-off wide, low hero angle, full body. Background: dim smoky Harlem ballroom, tables and chandeliers, no spotlight. Style: rubber-hose nineteen-thirties, no text. Beat two — twelve seconds. Dance: shimmy and Charleston kick. Camera: slow push-in, full body to chest. Beat three — fourteen seconds. Dance: spin-and-freeze on the final downbeat. Camera: slow orbit around the freeze.

props.title: Phase 2 — 3 dance beats (120 BPM swing, rubber-hose Calloway)
props.code:
BEAT B01  (15s)
Character: tall lanky grinning rubber-hose puppet, white zoot suit
Dance:     Calloway strut — glides side to side, head bobbing,
           snaps a finger hard on every downbeat
Camera:    locked-off wide, low hero angle, full body
BG:        dim smoky Harlem ballroom, tables + chandeliers,
           no spotlight, no stage, no confetti
Style:     rubber-hose 1930s cartoon, on the beat,
           no text, no extra characters

BEAT B02  (12s)
Dance:     shimmy + Charleston kick — weight swings hip to hip,
           knees snap out on the off-beat
Camera:    slow push-in, full body → chest
BG:        same ballroom

BEAT B03  (14s)
Dance:     spin-and-freeze on the final downbeat
Camera:    slow orbit around the freeze
BG:        same ballroom

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one. Audio is the master clock. Not the video length, not a fixed beat count. Librosa reads the real downbeats from the waveform; segments snap to those downbeats so every cut lands exactly where the music lands. The audio slice fed to Seedance is the signal it dances to. Change the song and the whole segment grid rebuilds from the new waveform.

props.heading: The downbeat grid is the cut grid.
props.body: analyze_audio.py runs librosa beat_track on the master WAV → beat_data.json with downbeat timestamps. build_dance_beatsheet.py greedy-packs beats to ≤15s ending on a downbeat — never over, because Seedance's audio cap is 15s. Each beat's audio slice is fed to Seedance via --audio: that is the signal Seedance locks motion to. The waveform IS the clock.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two. The background must be stated explicitly — not a mood, an actual space. Vague words like moody backdrop tell the model nothing and it invents a stage with a spotlight and falling confetti. Describe the actual space and what's in it. A dim smoky Harlem ballroom, tables and chandeliers, no spotlight. That sentence is most of the creative work. The prompt is not a mood board.

props.heading: Name the space. Don't describe a mood.
props.body: The 4-part prompt formula: CHARACTER (one clause, the look) → DANCE (move + beat relationship) → CAMERA (one angle, varied per beat) → BACKGROUND (explicit room) → STYLE (one-line tag). Background is where most first attempts fail: 'moody backdrop' → model invents a stage with a spotlight and confetti. Fix: describe the actual space and what's in it. 'dim smoky Harlem ballroom, tables + chandeliers, no spotlight, no stage, no confetti.'
props.quote: Describe the actual space and what's in it.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three. Beat-match is the thing to verify, not assume. Seedance advertises audio-locked motion. That does not mean every generated clip actually lands on the beat. Gate one: test one segment before the full batch run. Compare the clip's motion-energy peaks to the beat timestamps in beat data dot json. Energetic is not on-beat.

props.heading: Test one beat. Measure. Then run the batch.
props.body: Gate 1 in the pipeline: generate ONE beat with generate_video_seedance.sh, then compare motion-energy peaks to beat_data.json timestamps. 'Energetic' is not 'on-beat' — a clip can have high motion that doesn't align to the actual downbeats. This is the model's advertised strength AND the most common place it bluffs. Verify before burning the full batch.
props.quote: Beat-match is the thing to verify, not assume.

beats.narration_text: Verdict. Claude, Dancing. dance-video turns a song and a character into a beat-synced reel — no video editor, no timeline scrubbing. The four-part prompt is the free-phase deliverable. The downbeat grid is the clock. Seedance locks motion to audio. Verify one beat before the full run.

artifactLines: song + character → beat-synced dance reel, no video editor
4-part prompt (CHARACTER + DANCE + CAMERA + BACKGROUND) = free-phase output
downbeat grid rebuilt from the WAV — change song, grid rebuilds
Gate 1: verify one beat's motion peaks match the beat timestamps

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run dance-video against any danceable song. Two questions before you start: what does your dancer look like — describe their look in one clause. And what is the background space — an actual room, not a mood.

props.runningText: character look? background space — actual room?
output: What does your dancer look like — one clause?
What is the background — an actual space, not a mood?
Gate 1: test one beat before the full batch

beats.narration_text: Claude, Dancing. I'm Liam, in for Bear.
